body.title: Versioned engrams — version control for graph-native code
body.result: Delta-based change tracking for engram nodes. _compute_engram_delta() tracks narrative/cells/code_ref changes. Carried in version snapshot metadata. 5 new tests.
body.description:
Add version control to engrams (graph-native code: habits, procedures, traversal programs). Distinct from versioned memories because engrams are executable — version history is also an execution history.

## Motivation
Engrams are programs that Igor writes and edits himself. When he modifies a habit through self-experimentation, the old version is lost. Versioning enables:
- Rollback if a self-edit degrades performance
- A/B comparison between habit versions
- Audit trail of self-programming evolution
- Training signal: which edits improved outcomes?

## Distinct from T-versioned-memories
- Memories are data; engrams are code
- Engram versions need execution metadata (run count, success rate per version)
- Engram rollback has behavioral consequences — it changes what Igor does
- Version diffs for engrams are semantic (what changed in the logic) not just textual

## Open questions (explore with Igor)
- Does Igor want to reason about his own edit history?
- Should failed experiments auto-rollback to the previous engram version?
- How does this interact with the A/B graduation framework?
- Storage: version chain in metadata? Separate engram_versions table?

## Depends on
- T-versioned-memories (shared versioning infrastructure, if any)
